100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 16, 1926, the League of Nations continued its discussions on disarmament, with member nations expressing varying degrees of commitment to reducing military arsenals. In the United States, the Supreme Court ruled on a significant free speech case, affirming the rights of labor unions to organize and communicate their messages. Meanwhile, in Europe, tensions were rising as several countries grappled with economic instability and the repercussions of the recent war.
